paper.title: Adam: A Method for Stochastic Optimization

claims.text: Adam converges faster than SGD and other first-order methods on logistic regression, MLPs, and CNNs.
Adam requires little hyperparameter tuning; default settings (alpha=0.001, beta1=0.9, beta2=0.999) work well across problems.
Adam's parameter updates are invariant to diagonal rescaling of the gradients.

methods.name: Adam optimizer
methods.description: First-order optimizer using bias-corrected exponential moving averages of gradient and squared gradient: m_t, v_t with decay beta1/beta2; update theta -= alpha * m_hat / (sqrt(v_hat) + eps).
methods.runnable_hint: Implement Adam and SGD from scratch in numpy; train logistic regression on a synthetic dataset; compare training loss curves over epochs. Metric: final training loss for Adam vs SGD.
